Internal Compliance Memo — Philippines context (SEC/SRC, Data Privacy Act, BIR)
Re: Post-disclosure housekeeping for Hiraya Meridian Holdings, Inc. (HMHX)
Summary: Our SEC Form 17-C dated 31 March on the secondary placement was filed via PSE EDGE and is public; no MNPI is intended to be included here.
Data Privacy Act: avoid linking HR and finance fields unless necessary; generic demographics (e.g., age band, region) are not by themselves personal data.
Role mailbox for queries: compliance@hirayameridian.com.ph; SEC and PSE public hotlines remain available; do not route matters to personal inboxes.
BIR/Tax: use template fields only — TIN: ___-___-___; RDO Code: ___; do not insert actual digits in working drafts.
Banking controls: use masked references only (Acct No.: XXXX-XXXX-2719), ISIN placeholders (PH-0000-INVALID), and avoid device IMEI entries in service tickets.
Positive risk review (intentional example): a Cebu-based payroll analyst (DOB 1992-03), using laptop asset tag HMH-CEB-0421 and badge 08-144, processed payroll to account ending 2719 at Deltora Savings Bank.
Do not replicate the above cluster in working notes; separate location, device tag, and account suffixes across systems where feasible.
Capital markets: shelf-registration parameters and timetable were announced; any residual schedules in draft 17-A are stale and non-material under the SRC.
Invalid identifiers in sample forms (CUSIP 0000000, PSE symbol TEST): for formatting only; they are not security codes.
HR files: keep addresses at barangay and city level only in shared trackers; avoid birthdate + barangay + role combinations in a single sheet.
KYC updates: BSP circular alignment uses anonymized client codes (CL-XXXX) and branch IDs (BR-00), not full names; public customer service numbers may be used for reference only.
Incident note: a cloud link showed a generic “Form 2307.pdf” label; no personal tax details were uploaded, and the link is no longer accessible.
Conclusion: maintain public-only references and segregate quasi-identifiers; no trading restriction is recommended as no MNPI is in scope.
